perturbation.original_text: (g) "Work Product" means any and all Work and other items in any form that Turpin, solely or with others, conceives of, modifies, develops, contributes to, or reduces to practice during the period of Turpin's employment with the Company and which: (i) relate, directly or indirectly, to the Company's present or future foreseeable Business or Research and Development; or (ii) result from any work performed by Turpin for the Company; whether or not such are made during or after working hours, on or off the Company's premises.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) "Work Product" means any and all Inventions and other items in any form that Turpin, solely or with others, conceives of, modifies, develops, contributes to, or reduces to practice during the period of Turpin's employment with the Company and which: (i) relate, directly or indirectly, to the Company's present or future foreseeable Business or Research and Development; or (ii) result from any work performed by Turpin for the Company; whether or not such are made during or after working hours, on or off the Company's premises.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Work' with 'Inventions' in the definition of 'Work Product' creates a contradiction. 'Work' is defined as relating to the Business, including Software, records, documents, photographs, etc., while 'Inventions' is not explicitly defined in that section. This creates a conflict because later clauses referring to 'Work Product' now ambiguously refer to either all 'Work' (as initially defined) or specifically 'Inventions' which are not clearly laid out, leading to confusion about what Turpin is assigning to the Company. The ambiguity arises in section 2, which deals with ownership by the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership by the Company. Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Work Product' included 'Work', which had a broad scope encompassing various forms of output. The changed text narrows the definition of 'Work Product' to include only 'Inventions'. Because 'Inventions' isn't expressly defined as including the full scope of the original 'Work', this creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction in Section 2 ('Ownership by the Company'). The company's claim to ownership of all 'Work Product' is now unclear, as it's uncertain whether this refers to the narrower set of 'Inventions' or the original broader set of 'Work'. This directly impacts the enforceability of the ownership clause because it creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes about which outputs Turpin is assigning to the company. This satisfies the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Turpin agrees to maintain at all times adequate and current records relating to the creation and development of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, which records and all copies thereof shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the Company, and to disclose all such records and copies to the Company promptly.
perturbation.changed_text: Turpin agrees to maintain at all times adequate and current documentation relating to the creation and development of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, which documentation and all copies thereof shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the Company, and to disclose all such records and copies to the Company promptly.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'records' to 'documentation' in Section 3 introduces a contradiction. 'Records' are included in the definition of Work in section 1, while 'documentation' is a broader term that is not explicitly defined and can include any sort of documents, not just the records as stated in Section 1. This makes it uncertain whether Turpin is obligated to maintain and disclose *all* documents or only the 'records' initially specified within 'Work'. This change creates ambiguity in Section 6, which deals with the return of company property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Company Property. Turpin shall deliver to the Company all Work Product and Company Property, including all originals and copies thereof, in Turpin's possession and/or control, at the request of the Company, or, in the absence of such a request, upon the termination of Turpin's employment with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'records' to 'documentation' introduces ambiguity regarding what Turpin must return to the Company. The original text clearly specifies 'records' as part of 'Work Product'. The changed text uses 'documentation,' a broader term not explicitly defined as part of 'Work Product' and not explicitly synonymous with "records" which is defined in Section 1. This creates a contradiction with the 'Return of Company Property' clause, as it becomes unclear whether Turpin is required to return all documentation or only the 'records' that fall within the original definition of 'Work Product.' This ambiguity directly impacts the enforceability of the 'Return of Company Property' clause, fulfilling the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction due to Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason. Termination under this Section 12 shall be effective from the date written notice is delivered or the date specified in the written notice. whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's engagement with the Company cease for any reason. Termination under this Section 12 shall be effective from the date written notice is delivered or the date specified in the written notice. whichever is later.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'employment' to 'engagement' in Section 7 creates a potential in-text contradiction. The agreement consistently refers to Turpin's 'employment' throughout. Changing it to 'engagement' introduces ambiguity: does this include consultancy or other non-employee relationships? If Turpin's relationship changes from employee to consultant, does this trigger termination rights? Because elsewhere the document references employement, it creates contradiction and uncertainty in the enforceability of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Employment by Affiliates. Turpin acknowledges and agrees that Turpin's employment with the Company may be succeeded by employment with a Company Affiliate, in which case the terms of this Agreement shall continue in effect with respect to such employment until an agreement relating to this subject matter is signed between Turpin and the Company Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'employment' with 'engagement'. While the original agreement consistently uses 'employment,' the shift to 'engagement' creates ambiguity. The contradicted text specifically references 'Turpin's employment,' indicating a specific type of relationship. The change introduces a potential contradiction: if Turpin's relationship changes from employee to consultant (an 'engagement'), does this trigger termination rights under Section 12, given that the contradicted text focuses solely on 'employment'? This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
